    Beginner Version Of Jumping Jacks
    YouTube · WeShape

    20 Jan 2026

    Beginner Version Of Jumping Jacks

    YouTube·WeShape·20 Jan 2026
    YouTube
    0:18
    Jumping jacks are a fun way to get your heart rate up! It only takes a few secs and here are some variations for you to try and I included one that is low impact. 1️⃣ Lateral to Front Raise Jack: keep your back straight and core engaged while you move your arms. Prevent yourself from leaning back. 2️⃣ Jack Squat : while you squat be sure to keep your weight pressed into your heels . Keep the knees forward, chest up and head straight. 3️⃣ Cross Jack: this one takes a bit of coordination but is a fun one once you get it. Cross the legs as you cross your arms, open up and then switch the legs and arms in front. You can always start a bit slower. 4️⃣ Side Tap Jack: this one is low impact. Tap the foot out to the side as you lift the arm out. 5️⃣ Weighted Jack: one of my favourites as I incorporate this into many of my classes. Again, keep the back straight and core engaged. You can try all of these at 30 secs intervals and add into any of your workouts! Enjoy!
    Instagram · Cindy Yu | Online Trainer
    0:14
    JUMPING JACKS
    Pinterest · krikar_official
    0:05
    Jumping Jacks for Quick Cardio 💪 Full Body Workout!
    YouTube · Performance Fit Pilates
    Performance Fit Pilates
    YouTube·
    7 Feb 2025
    0:11
    The Major Health Benefits of Jumping Jacks ✨ Jumping jacks may seem like a simple, old-school exercise, but don't underestimate their power! This no-equipment, full-body workout is one of the easiest and most effective ways to boost your fitness anytime, anywhere. 💚 Why You Should Be Doing Jumping Jacks: Improves Mobility & Flexibility The dynamic movement helps keep your joints loose and active, promoting better range of motion. Boosts Blood Circulation Just a few minutes gets your blood flowing, energizing your entire body and brain. Supports Heart Health Jumping jacks are a great cardio workout, strengthening your heart and improving endurance. Enhances Coordination & Balance Engaging your arms and legs at the same time helps improve body coordination and stability. Burns Calories Quickly A perfect way to get your heart rate up and torch calories ideal for quick home workouts. Trains the Frontal Plane This side-to-side motion targets muscles often missed in traditional forward-and-back exercises. No Equipment, No Excuses You don't need a gym, fancy gear, or lots of space. Just a few minutes of jumping jacks each day can make a big difference in your fitness journey.
    Instagram · Rashmika Boruah

    People also ask
    What is a jumping jack good for?
    An AI Overview is not available for this searchCan't generate an AI overview right now. Try again later.
    AI Overview
    Jumping jacks are an excellent, zero-equipment, full-body exercise. They are highly effective for boosting cardiovascular endurance, burning calories, increasing bone density, and improving overall coordination. Because they work multiple muscle groups at once, they serve as a fantastic warm-up or quick conditioning routine. 
    The Core Benefits 
    • Cardiovascular Health: They get your heart pumping quickly, improving blood circulation, heart health, and lung capacity. 
    • Calorie Burning & Weight Management: Acting as an energetic plyometric move, jumping jacks accelerate your metabolic rate and burn roughly 8 to 12 calories per minute, making them great for fat loss. 
    • Bone Density: As a weight-bearing and anti-gravity exercise, they apply stress to the bones with each landing, which helps increase bone mass and prevents conditions like osteoporosis. 
    • Total Body Toning: They engage your entire body, targeting your calves, quadriceps, hamstrings, glutes, core, shoulders, and back muscles. 
    • Coordination & Mobility: Moving opposite limbs simultaneously sharpens your balance, agility, and rhythm. The side-to-side (frontal plane) motion also helps keep your shoulder and hip joints mobile. 
    • Post-Meal Glucose Control: Short bursts of jumping jacks after eating can increase muscle glucose uptake, helping to manage blood sugar spikes. 
    For tips on how to properly perform jumping jacks to ensure you get the most out of your workout:

      क्या जंपिंग जैक से बेली फैट बर्न होता है?

      How to Do Jumping Jacks


      WebMD
      https://www.webmd.com › ... › Reference
      WebMD
      https://www.webmd.com › ... › Reference
      The jumping jack is an effective way to stimulate all the vital muscles of your body. It is a full-body exercise used in cardio and warm-up sessions.Read more

      Jumping jacks are a fun way to get your heart rate up! It only takes a few secs and here are some variations for you to try and I included one that is low impact. 1️⃣ Lateral to Front Raise Jack: keep your back straight and core engaged while you move your arms. Prevent yourself from leaning back. 2️⃣ Jack Squat : while you squat be sure to keep your weight pressed into your heels . Keep the knees forward, chest up and head straight. 3️⃣ Cross Jack: this one takes a bit of coordination but is a fun one once you get it. Cross the legs as you cross your arms, open up and then switch the legs and arms in front. You can always start a bit slower. 4️⃣ Side Tap Jack: this one is low impact. Tap the foot out to the side as you lift the arm out. 5️⃣ Weighted Jack: one of my favourites as I incorporate this into many of my classes. Again, keep the back straight and core engaged. You can try all of these at 30 secs intervals and add into any of your workouts! Enjoy!
